page: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-2xL4 is a GPU-accelerated server configured with 12 vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM. The processing power is driven by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor using the x86_64 architecture, featuring 12 physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio under a shared CPU allocation model. Acceleration is provided by two NVIDIA L4 GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering a combined 48 GB of VRAM. This instance does not include local storage.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scoring in the top 10%. Multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth fall into the average tier, though multi-threaded bzip3 decompression shows strong performance in the top 25%. For AI workloads, LLM text generation using a small 135M model reaches top-tier speeds of 349.81 tokens per second, while prompt processing remains in the average tier.

The server offers qualitative cost efficiency by bundling dual enterprise-grade GPUs with a substantial 128 GB memory footprint, making it suitable for memory-intensive parallel processing. However, the shared CPU allocation and lack of local storage represent key architectural tradeoffs that require external storage solutions. This configuration is optimized for workloads such as machine learning inference, data science jobs, and GPU-accelerated rendering.
description: The UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-2xL4 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 12 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9575F processor and 128 GB of RAM. It integrates two NVIDIA L4 GPUs from the Ada Lovelace architecture, providing 48 GB of total VRAM, but does not include local storage. Operating on a shared CPU allocation model under KVM virtualization, the server delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and high-speed single-threaded compression. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are average, while LLM text generation for small models achieves top-tier throughput. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for parallel computing tasks, though the shared CPU allocation and absence of local storage are notable tradeoffs. It is best suited for machine learning inference, data science, and GPU-accelerated rendering workloads.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-2xL4 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 12 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and two NVIDIA L4 GPUs with 48 GB VRAM, optimized for machine learning inference and data science.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-2xL4 features 12 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and dual NVIDIA L4 GPUs for accelerated machine learning and data science workloads.
tagline: A dual-GPU accelerated server combining AMD EPYC processing and Ada Lovelace architecture for memory-intensive parallel computing and machine learning inference.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA L4 GPUs with 48 GB total VRAM for parallel acceleration
12 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 9575F processor on x86_64 architecture
128 GB of system memory supporting memory-intensive applications
Top-tier single-core CPU and small-model LLM text generation performance
Shared CPU allocation model under KVM virtualization without local storage
Optimized for machine learning inference, data science, and rendering
